name: Houston Police
description_short: Houston Police Federal Credit Union is a law enforcement-focused credit union established in 1937, serving over 31,600 members with $907.7M in assets.
description_long:
Houston Police Federal Credit Union was established in 1937 and is headquartered in Houston, Texas. As a member-owned cooperative financial institution, it serves a specific membership base tied to law enforcement with a primary mission to provide tailored financial services to this community.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including checking and savings accounts, credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRA/KEOGH accounts, wire transfers, and bill pay services. Members also have access to online and mobile banking platforms with features like remote deposit capture, e-statements, electronic signature authentication, and mobile payments. The institution maintains 1 physical branch location at 1600 Memorial Drive with 3 ATMs in the network and provides both lobby (open until 5:00 PM) and drive-thru (open until 6:00 PM) services.

Houston Police FCU distinguishes itself through its membership exclusivity, serving law enforcement professionals and related groups with common bonds. The credit union manages substantial assets ($907.7M) and maintains a dedicated staff of over 91 employees. Member reviews are mixed, with an average rating of 3.4/5 stars. Positive reviews highlight staff professionalism and helpfulness during difficult financial times, while negative reviews cite restrictions on card usage for travel and occasional service issues.

The credit union is best suited for law enforcement personnel in the Houston area seeking member-focused banking services with personalized customer support. However, prospective members should note membership eligibility requirements based on employment or affiliation with law enforcement, potential card usage restrictions, and the limited branch network compared to larger financial institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking Accounts (Share Draft accounts with 22,400+ members)
Mobile Payments and Electronic Signature Authentication
pros: Specialized focus on law enforcement community with tailored financial services
Strong asset base of $907.7M providing financial stability
Comprehensive digital banking platform with online banking, mobile app, and remote deposit capture
Substantial membership base of 31,600+ members indicating operational maturity
Staff rated as professional and helpful in member reviews
Full suite of lending products including auto loans and personal loans
IRA/KEOGH accounts and certificate of deposit options for retirement planning
cons: Limited branch network with only 1 physical location and 3 ATMs in service area
Membership restrictions based on law enforcement affiliation exclude general public
Credit card reported by members as having travel usage restrictions
Mixed member satisfaction with 3.4/5 average rating indicating service inconsistencies
Limited geographic reach confined to Houston, Texas area
best_for: Law enforcement officers and affiliated personnel in the Houston area
Police department employees seeking member-focused banking relationships
Houston-based law enforcement families looking for community-focused financial services
Officers seeking specialized lending products tailored to their employment profile
diagnosis: Houston Police Federal Credit Union is best for law enforcement officers and affiliated personnel in Houston seeking personalized banking services within a community-focused institution. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted to law enforcement with specific common bonds, and the single branch location limits accessibility compared to traditional banks.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
